name: Rownd Authentication API
what_it_does: Authentication platform with REST API focused on frictionless no-redirect sign-in flows — supporting magic links, passkeys, Google One Tap, and Apple Sign-In with in-context authentication (no full-page redirect), plus user data management and profile sync.
use_cases: Adding passwordless authentication to mobile and web apps without redirect flows
Managing user profiles and custom data fields via User API
Implementing Google One Tap or Apple Sign-In without full OAuth redirect cycle
Building auth flows that feel native to the app with in-context widget approach
Syncing user data and custom fields programmatically via Management API
not_for: B2B SaaS with complex organization management and SSO (use Kinde or WorkOS)
Enterprise identity with SCIM provisioning and governance
Teams needing on-premise or self-hosted authentication deployment
best_when: A mobile or consumer app needs frictionless no-redirect authentication with magic links, passkeys, and social login — prioritizing UX over B2B features.
avoid_when: You need multi-tenant organizations, enterprise SSO, RBAC, or complex permission systems — Rownd focuses on consumer-friendly auth UX.

auth.notes: App key + secret pair for server-side API calls. User JWT tokens issued by Rownd for client validation. Bearer token for Management API. App-scoped access — no fine-grained scopes.

pricing.paid_tiers: Growth from $25/month for 10,000+ MAU; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Very generous free tier. Focus on mobile-first and consumer apps. US-based. Good for bootstrapped products with consumer user base. No organization or RBAC features — explicitly consumer-focused.

performance.rate_limits: Standard API rate limits per plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Management API covers users, user data fields, and app configuration. JWT validation for user tokens. Webhook support for auth events. SDK-first approach — REST API is secondary to SDKs. Smaller ecosystem than Auth0 or Clerk.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User lookup idempotent by identifier. User data updates idempotent for same values. Magic link sends create new tokens — not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: No-redirect auth flow requires in-context widget rendering — not compatible with pure API auth
Management API is secondary to SDK — some features only exposed via SDKs
User JWT short-lived — must refresh; server-to-server token exchange differs from user tokens
Limited RBAC — custom user data fields are unstructured rather than typed permission system

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. JWT-based user tokens. No password storage — passwordless by design reduces attack surface. App secret server-side only. US data residency.
